stages.stage: Fault: links l12 (s1→s2) and l30 (s3→s0) both failed

invariants.raw_output:
====================================================================
  4-Node Compute Cluster — Structural Invariant Monitor Demo
  Boundary Logic Pipeline  |  boundary_logic_python v0.1.0
====================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 1 — Nominal: 4-node cluster (s0─s1─s2─s3─s0)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  system   : cluster_nominal
  cells    : dim0:4, dim1:4
  Betti    : [1, 1]
  sha256   : db87afe8d3ad28ef...
  valid ∂² : True

  Interpretation:
    b₀=1 → fully connected (all nodes reachable)
    b₁=1 → one ring path (ring routing valid)
  This payload is the certified structural baseline.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 2 — Fault: link l23 (s2→s3) has failed
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  system   : cluster_link_fault
  cells    : dim0:4, dim1:3
  Betti    : [1, 0]
  sha256   : 9589fa1ae583ea4e...
  valid ∂² : True

  Invariant delta vs nominal:
    b₀: 1 → 1  (no change)
    b₁: 1 → 0  ← VIOLATION

  Monitor advisories:
  🟡 [WARNING] CLUSTER_RING_VIOLATED
     Ring path count dropped from 1 to 0. Ring routing assumption violated.
     → Switch to fallback mesh routing. Flag failed link(s) for replacement. Revalidate routing tables before resuming ring-dependent workloads.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 3 — Fault: links l12 (s1→s2) and l30 (s3→s0) both failed
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  system   : cluster_partition
  cells    : dim0:4, dim1:2
  Betti    : [2, 0]
  sha256   : 0ba86f74c32f6aef...
  valid ∂² : True

  Invariant delta vs nominal:
    b₀: 1 → 2  ← CRITICAL VIOLATION
    b₁: 1 → 0  ← VIOLATION

  Monitor advisories:
  🔴 [CRITICAL] CONNECTIVITY_PARTITION
     Cluster partitioned into 2 isolated islands (was 1). Some nodes are topologically unreachable.
     → Isolate affected partition. Halt cross-island workloads. Trigger failover for affected services. Restore physical link or provision backup path.
  🟡 [WARNING] CLUSTER_RING_VIOLATED
     Ring path count dropped from 1 to 0. Ring routing assumption violated.
     → Switch to fallback mesh routing. Flag failed link(s) for replacement. Revalidate routing tables before resuming ring-dependent workloads.

====================================================================
  Pipeline summary
====================================================================

  The Betti number sequence b=[b₀, b₁] is the structural certificate.

  Nominal          b=[1,1]  — cluster intact, ring routing valid
  Link fault       b=[1,0]  — ring broken, reroute required
  Partition        b=[2,0]  — cluster split, cross-island traffic blocked

  Each payload is SHA-256 signed.

  Key property: the invariant computation is algebraically exact.
  b₁=1 is not a heuristic — it is a theorem about the declared topology.
  When b₁ drops to 0, the ring path provably no longer exists.
